outcome_contract.why: Right now, if someone accidentally deletes the IP allowlist setting, the entire admin tool becomes publicly accessible with no warning. Session tokens also cannot be revoked before their 7-day expiry, meaning a stolen cookie remains valid for up to a week.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: IP allowlist rejects by default when unconfigured; session revocation mechanism exists via KV-backed minimum-issuedAt check; session verification is fully timing-safe.

engineering_coverage.applicable: Runtime warning on coherence mismatch. Deny-all default covers the primary gap.
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Deploy-time block on missing secret + runtime deny-all default. Stronger pre-deploy safety.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Option B provides earlier detection of the most common failure mode (missing secret).

engineering_coverage.applicable: Same: `uploaderLog()` for security events. Runtime coherence warning is also a log event.
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Same: `uploaderLog()` for security events. Preflight outputs structured pass/fail.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Both options use the same logging seam.

engineering_coverage.applicable: Unit test for runtime coherence check in `accessControl.test.ts`.
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Unit test for deny-all; integration test for preflight (preflight already has test patterns).
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Option B has a slightly broader test surface but follows existing patterns.

engineering_coverage.applicable: Same: KV key `xa:revocation:min_issued_at` for revocation timestamp. Token format unchanged.
Same: one KV read per authenticated request for revocation. Runtime coherence check is negligible (two env var reads per request).
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Same: one KV read per authenticated request for revocation. Runtime coherence check fires once per process via deduplication (negligible cost). Preflight check adds zero per-request cost.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Both options include a runtime coherence check. Option B deduplicates it to once-per-process, making the per-request overhead effectively zero for both. No meaningful performance difference.

engineering_coverage.applicable: Must set `XA_UPLOADER_ALLOWED_IPS` secret before deploy. Coherence mismatch detected only after deploy.
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Must set `XA_UPLOADER_ALLOWED_IPS` secret before deploy. Preflight blocks deploy if secret is missing.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Option B is safer for rollout — catches the missing-secret case before deploy.

open_operator_questions: Should max session age be reduced from 7 days, and if so to what value?
What IP addresses should be in the production allowlist?

stage_payload.decision_summary: The fact-find identified two substantive security gaps in the xa-uploader admin tool: (1) the IP allowlist defaults to allow-all when `XA_UPLOADER_ALLOWED_IPS` is unset, silently exposing the catalog admin API to the internet, and (2) session tokens have a 7-day lifetime with no revocation mechanism. Two additional items (timing leak on version check, security event logging) are lower-priority improvements to ship in the same pass. The analysis must decide between implementation approaches and resolve how to implement allowlist/proxy-trust coherence validation. A key constraint: `XA_UPLOADER_A
goals: Change IP allowlist default from allow-all to deny-all.
Implement session revocation via KV-backed minimum-issuedAt check.
Fix timing leak in version check (cosmetic).
Add security event logging via existing `uploaderLog()`.
non_goals: CSP tightening (acceptable for internal admin tool).
assumptions: KV reads are sub-ms in Cloudflare (well-documented platform characteristic).
Operator accesses the admin tool infrequently (admin-only workflow).

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: Primary objective — close the two substantive gaps
Deny-all default is a breaking change; must not lock operator out
Fewer moving parts = fewer defects in security-critical code
Existing sessions must not break during transition
Security events need an audit trail
Security changes require strong test evidence

options_considered.Description: All changes in one pass. Coherence check at runtime in `accessControl.ts` (log warning when allowlist is non-empty but proxy trust is disabled).
options_considered.Upside: Simple to implement; all security work ships together; runtime check catches misconfiguration regardless of deploy path.
options_considered.Downside: Runtime check only fires after traffic hits the Worker; no deploy-time safety net for coherence specifically.
options_considered.Key risks: Coherence misconfiguration discovered only at request time, not deploy time.

options_considered.Description: All changes in one pass. Promote `XA_UPLOADER_ALLOWED_IPS` from optional `[vars]` to a required Cloudflare secret; add to `XA_UPLOADER_REQUIRED_CF_SECRET_NAMES` so preflight enforces its existence. Runtime code treats missing var as deny-all. Runtime once-per-process coherence warning when allowlist is non-empty but proxy trust is disabled.
options_considered.Upside: Deploy-time safety: preflight fails if the secret is missing, catching the most common misconfiguration before deploy. Aligns with existing preflight pattern.
options_considered.Downside: Config model change: var -> secret. Must provision in all environments including preview, or preview preflight fails. Cannot validate the secret's value (only existence).
options_considered.Key risks: CI/environment setup burden; empty-but-present secret edge case; proxy trust still needs a runtime check.

options_considered.Option: C: Keep allowlist in `[vars]` + preflight value check
options_considered.Description: Keep `XA_UPLOADER_ALLOWED_IPS` as a non-secret `wrangler.toml [vars]` entry (its current form). Preflight reads and validates both the value and proxy-trust coherence at deploy time.
options_considered.Upside: Full deploy-time validation of both existence, value, and coherence — preflight can read `[vars]` values unlike secrets.
options_considered.Downside: `[vars]` values are checked into source control, which means allowlist IPs are visible in the repo. Less secure than promoting to a secret. Does not align with Option B's direction of treating the allowlist as mandatory secret-grade config.
options_considered.Key risks: Repo-visible IPs (minor privacy concern); weaker config posture than secret promotion.

chosen_approach.Recommendation: Option B — Preflight existence check with single-pass implementation, supplemented by a once-per-process runtime coherence log warning.
chosen_approach.KV namespace production ID placeholder (: 23`) must be resolved before revocation features deploy. The preflight already validates KV namespace configuration.
chosen_approach.Q: What IP addresses should be in the production allowlist?
chosen_approach.Why operator input is required: Only the operator knows which IPs they access the admin tool from.
chosen_approach.Planning impact: Must be set as a Cloudflare secret before deploying the deny-all default. The build itself does not depend on the specific values.

rejected_approaches: **Option A (runtime coherence only)** — Rejected because it discovers the most common failure mode (missing allowlist) only after traffic hits the Worker. The deny-all default prevents actual exposure, but operator discovers the lockout after deploy rather than before. Since the preflight seam already exists, the trade-off (promoting to required secret + provisioning in all environments) is worth the deploy-time safety net.

**Option C (keep in `[vars]` with value check)** — Rejected because it keeps the allowlist as a non-secret var visible in source control, which is a weaker config posture than promoting to a secret. While Option C offers full deploy-time value validation (preflight can read vars, unlike secrets), the combination of preflight existence check + runtime deny-all default + runtime coherence warning in Option B achieves equivalent safety with a stronger config model.

sequencing_constraints: Task 1 (deny-all + preflight + coherence warning) can ship independently — no KV dependency. Delivers the full chosen approach for IP allowlist hardening.
Task 2 (revocation) depends on KV namespace production ID being resolved.
Task 3 (logging) and Task 4 (timing) have no dependencies and can be parallelized with Task 2.

Operator must provision `XA_UPLOADER_ALLOWED_IPS` as a Cloudflare secret in production and preview environments (with actual IPs — no wildcard) before deploying Task 1. `XA_TRUST_PROXY_IP_HEADERS=1` is already set in `wrangler.toml` for both production and preview environments.
risks_to_carry_forward: KV namespace placeholder ID must be resolved before Task 2 ships.
Deny-all default is a breaking change — operator must configure secrets before deploy.
Runtime coherence warning fires after deploy; preflight catches only secret existence.
